title: Three-Layer Memory System — Jake's working convention for persistence
summary: Jake's convention for how he wants collaborative memory to work. Three layers: (1) Claude's memory system (persistent, lossy, vibe), (2) project files (durable within a project), (3) Jake's local filesystem (ground truth, OS-timestamped, inspectable, portable). Local filesystem is the anchor. Each insight is saved as its own .md file with a descriptive slug (no dates — OS tracks). Every file has TWO sections: Raw (Jake's stream-of-thought, verbatim) and Polished (Claude's refinement). Neither is canonical; both get preserved as corpus. Self-reference note: the method itself previews the thesis (nothing finalized, only iterated). This IS how the infinity_problem directory is structured.
